_comment: Drop-in tool definitions for OpenAI function calling, Anthropic tool_use, and Gemini function declarations. Same schema works across all three — just adapt the wrapper format per provider.

tools.name: resume_session
tools.description: Get a compact 'where we left off' digest: the status board (live entity state), the last checkpoints (most recent actions taken), staged work in flight, recently updated memories, and critical rules that always apply. This is the authoritative record of what happened in previous sessions. Use judgment: any request that presupposes shared history — continuing work, 'the pending tasks', resuming after a break — cannot be answered from the conversation alone. Cheap to call; prefer checking over assuming.

tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Search your persistent memory store for facts, rules, and lessons relevant to the current task. CALL THIS at the start of every session, before answering questions about past work, before applying preferences the user may have stated, or whenever you are unsure whether you have prior context on a topic. SCOPED BY DEFAULT: results come from the current project's family plus global memories — pass all_projects=true to search the entire store (each hit then carries its 'project' label), or project=<label> to hard-filter one. Returns memories ranked by relevance — only what matters, not everything. Superseded (corrected/replaced) memories are hidden by default. Topics advertised with counts in the resume digest are guaranteed to return results here.

query.description: Natural-language description of what you want to recall. Examples: 'user preferences for code style', 'how does the Instagram pipeline work', 'what trading rules should I follow'

top_k.description: Max results to return (default 8, max 30). Use 15-20 for broad topic discovery.

type_filter.description: fb=feedback, us=user, pj=project, rf=reference, cn=convention, lx=lesson, co=core (per-project operating guide), tr=tracker (LIVE status of one entity, slug '<entity>-status', updated by re-saving the same slug; memgit is the authority for this status, files are downstream)

include_superseded.description: Also score memories that a newer memory supersedes (hidden by default).

project.description: Hard-filter to exactly one project's memories. Usually unnecessary — searches are already scoped to the current project's family + global.

all_projects.description: Search the ENTIRE store instead of the default scope (current project family + global). Every hit carries its 'project' label.

tools.name: get_memory
tools.description: Fetch a single memory by its exact slug identifier. CALL THIS when search_memories returned a relevant slug and you need the full details — why the rule exists, when to apply it, related tags. Slugs are kebab-case like 'ig-pipeline-no-fallback' or 'trading-capital-track'.

tools.name: save_memory
tools.description: Persist a new fact, rule, preference, or lesson to the memory store for future sessions. CALL THIS whenever you learn something the user would want you to remember next time — a preference they stated, a rule they corrected you on, a project decision, a lesson from a mistake, or a reference to an external system. Do NOT save ephemeral or task-specific details; save durable facts only. If a memory with the same slug exists, this updates it. To update a 'tr' tracker (live entity status), re-save the same '<entity>-status' slug. When this memory corrects existing ones, pass their slugs in 'supersedes'.

slug.description: Short kebab-case identifier, unique per memory. Examples: 'user-prefers-terse-responses', 'ig-pipeline-no-fallback', 'trading-confirm-only-orders'. Use the same slug to update an existing memory.

rule.description: The primary fact or rule to remember (max ~200 chars). Write as a declarative statement: 'always X', 'never Y', 'Z is located at ...'.

type_code.description: fb=feedback, us=user, pj=project, rf=reference, cn=convention, lx=lesson, co=core (per-project operating guide), tr=tracker (LIVE status of one entity, slug '<entity>-status', updated by re-saving the same slug; memgit is the authority for this status, files are downstream)

why.description: Why this rule exists — the incident, reason, or motivation. Optional but strongly recommended so future sessions understand the context.

when.description: When or where to apply this rule. Optional.

tags.description: Topic tags for search filtering. Examples: ['instagram', 'trading', 'admin']

priority.description: 1=low (background context), 2=medium (apply when relevant, default), 3=critical (always loaded into every session)

supersedes.description: Slugs of memories this one REPLACES (corrections, updated decisions). Pass this instead of 'CORRECTED:' prefixes — superseded memories stop surfacing automatically.

related.description: Slugs of related memories (cross-reference, not replacement).

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: List all memories, returning slug + rule for each (compact view). CALL THIS to browse what is stored, discover memory slugs, or audit the full memory set. For finding relevant memories on a specific topic, use search_memories instead — it is faster and ranked by relevance.

type_filter.description: fb=feedback, us=user, pj=project, rf=reference, cn=convention, lx=lesson, co=core (per-project operating guide), tr=tracker (LIVE status of one entity, slug '<entity>-status', updated by re-saving the same slug; memgit is the authority for this status, files are downstream)

min_priority.description: Only return memories at or above this priority. 1=all, 2=medium and critical only, 3=critical only.

openai._comment: Wrap each tool as: { type: 'function', function: { name, description, parameters } }

anthropic._comment: Use tools directly: { name, description, input_schema: { type: 'object', properties, required } }

gemini._comment: Use FunctionDeclaration: { name, description, parameters: { type: 'OBJECT', properties, required } }. Enum values go in: { type: 'STRING', enum: [...] }
